message:
chore(beads): close bd-l8x9.4 PlasmaFx extraction to ftui-extras

Extracted plasma backdrop effect from demo to reusable library module:
- Created crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/plasma.rs
- Theme-aware palettes (Sunset, Ocean, Fire, Neon, Cyberpunk)
- Quality scaling (Full/Minimal wave functions)
- Comprehensive tests (determinism, tiny-area safety, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.13","title":"Baseline Profiling + Opportunity Matrix","description":"## Task
Collect baseline performance metrics, flamegraphs, and an opportunity matrix before changes.

## Scope
- Benchmarks at key sizes (80x24, 120x40).
- Capture CPU and allocation profiles.
- Score hotspots by impact and effort.

## Tests / Validation
- Store raw outputs and command lines in JSONL.
- Record p50/p95/p99 and compare after changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Baselines are reproducible.
- Opportunity matrix guides optimization work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.991490865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:19:15.138168417Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:19:15.138150193Z","close_reason":"Completed: baseline benchmarks captured for ftui-render and ftui-layout at 80x24/120x40/200x60. Created opportunity matrix with top 3 recommendations (set/set_raw gap, fill SIMD, layout caching). All artifacts saved to docs/profiling/.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.991490865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.14","title":"Golden Output Harness + Checksums","description":"Generate golden outputs and checksums for resize scenarios to prove isomorphism.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Golden Output Harness + Checksums
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.17","title":"Input Fairness + Scheduler Integration","description":"Ensure resize scheduling does not starve input/events; integrate fairness rules into main loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Input Fairness + Scheduler Integration
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.18","title":"Flicker/Tear Detection Harness","description":"Build detection harness to flag tearing/flicker (sync output gaps, partial clears) with verbose logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Flicker/Tear Detection Harness
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Correctness: add property tests for diff/present invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, no-ghosting on shrink)
- Golden outputs: checksum render outputs for canonical fixtures; ensure isomorphism under optimization
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

-{"id":"bd-32x8","title":"Async Validation: Deadline Controller (Survival + Expected Loss)","description":"## Goal
Choose whether to cancel or wait for an in-flight async validation using a principled, latency-aware decision rule.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Model validation duration T with an online survival distribution. Use hazard h(t) and expected remaining time to evaluate expected loss:
- Survival: S(t) = P(T > t)
- Hazard: h(t) = f(t) / S(t)
- Expected remaining time: E[T - t | T > t] = (1 / S(t)) * integral_{t}^{inf} S(u) du

Decision rule:
Loss(wait) = L_stale * P(T > deadline) + L_delay * E[T - t | T > t]
Loss(cancel) = L_false_invalid + L_recompute
Choose action with smaller expected loss.

## Implementation Notes
- Track completion times per validator (EMA + variance).
- Approximate with a simple parametric family:
  - Weibull: S(t) = exp(-(t/lambda)^k), h(t) = (k/lambda)*(t/lambda)^(k-1)
  - Log-normal if tails are heavy.
- Expose config for loss weights and deadline budget.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cancels only when expected loss of waiting exceeds cancel loss.
- Uses rolling window stats to adapt to slow validators.
- Never surfaces stale results after a newer input.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_cancel_when_long_tail: long-tail durations trigger cancel under tight deadline.
- unit_wait_when_fast: low-latency validator is not cancelled.
- unit_deadline_respected: validation state transitions to timeout cleanly.
- property_random_durations: decision matches expected-loss oracle within tolerance.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Simulated validators with controlled latency distributions; log decision + rationale.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:09:04.339712046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:05:42.801828632Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-33xi","title":"Layout solver: Max constraint wastes space when item hits limit","description":"The layout solver has a known issue where Max constraints waste available space. When an item hits its Max limit, the redistributed space calculation is incorrect. Repro test exists at crates/ftui-layout/src/repro_max_constraint.rs. Root cause: when Max constraint is violated, space accounting doesn't properly handle the difference between allocated shares and actual consumed space.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:18:38.173826768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T18:20:17.661678740Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T18:20:17.661659203Z","close_reason":"Test passes - bug appears to have been fixed in a previous commit","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-34lz","title":"Widget API E2E Test Script","description":"## Purpose

Create a comprehensive E2E test script for Widget API verification with detailed logging.

## Script: scripts/widget_api_e2e.sh

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-/tmp/widget_api_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

echo "=== Widget API E2E Test Suite ==="
echo "Log directory: $LOG_DIR"
echo "Started at: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo ""

# Step 1: Workspace Build
echo "[1/7] Building workspace..."
cargo build --workspace 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/01_build.log"
echo "✓ Build complete"

# Step 2: Unit Tests
echo "[2/7] Running unit tests..."
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/02_test.log"
echo "✓ Tests complete"

# Step 3: Clippy
echo "[3/7] Running clippy..."
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/03_clippy.log"
echo "✓ Clippy complete"

# Step 4: Feature Combinations
echo "[4/7] Testing feature combinations..."
for feature in canvas forms charts; do
  echo "  - ftui-extras --features $feature"
  cargo check -p ftui-extras --features $feature 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/04_features.log"
done
echo "✓ Features complete"

# Step 5: Documentation Build
echo "[5/7] Building documentation..."
cargo doc --workspace --no-deps 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/05_doc.log"
echo "✓ Docs complete"

# Step 6: Widget Signature Verification
echo "[6/7] Verifying Widget signatures..."
echo "Checking for old Buffer signatures..."
if rg 'fn render.*buf: &mut Buffer' crates/ftui-widgets/src/*.rs 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "ERROR: Found old Buffer signature\!"
  exit 1
fi
echo "Checking for new Frame signatures..."
rg 'fn render.*frame: &mut Frame' crates/ftui-widgets/src/*.rs | tee "$LOG_DIR/06_signatures.log"
echo "✓ All signatures use Frame"

# Step 7: Snapshot Tests
echo "[7/7] Running snapshot tests..."
cargo test -p ftui-harness --test widget_snapshots 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/07_snapshots.log"
echo "✓ Snapshots complete"

# Summary
echo ""
echo "=== E2E Test Suite Complete ==="
echo "Ended at: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo "Logs: $LOG_DIR/"
ls -la "$LOG_DIR/"
```

## Detailed Logging

Each step writes to a separate log file:
- `01_build.log` - Full cargo build output
- `02_test.log` - All test results with RUST_LOG=debug
- `03_clippy.log` - Clippy warnings/errors
- `04_features.log` - Feature combination checks
- `05_doc.log` - Documentation build output
- `06_signatures.log` - Widget signature grep results
- `07_snapshots.log` - Snapshot test results

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Script is executable and passes on current codebase
- [ ] All logs are captured with timestamps
- [ ] Exit code 0 only if ALL steps pass
